channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Get into med school in the graphic memoir 'See One, Do One, Teach One'
item.description: Grace Farris' book brings readers behind the med school scenes — documenting anxiety over loans, bouts of impostor syndrome, friendships forged in stress, and the ever-impossible work-life balance.
item.content:encoded: Grace Farris' book brings readers behind the med school scenes — documenting anxiety over loans, bouts of impostor syndrome, friendships forged in stress, and the ever-impossible work-life balance.
item.dc:creator: Tahneer Oksman

item.title: U.S. signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia. And, Nolan Wells' independent autopsy results
item.description: The U.S. has signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. And, the independent autopsy results of Nolan Wells fuel more questions surrounding his cause of death.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. And, the independent autopsy results of Nolan Wells fuel more questions surrounding his cause of death.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: This AI tool promises a 'second sight of eyes' to clinicians. Did patients benefit?
item.description: Medical workers in a Nairobi clinic used an AI tool to check their work. A new study evaluates the result.
item.content:encoded:
Medical workers in a Nairobi clinic used an AI tool to check their work. A new study evaluates the result.

(Image credit: Patrick Meinhardt/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: Why health officials do not have a good handle on the current Ebola outbreak
item.description: To get slightly ahead of the virus, health officials need to quickly identify people at risk of infection, a practice known as contact tracing. That's a challenge in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
item.content:encoded:
To get slightly ahead of the virus, health officials need to quickly identify people at risk of infection, a practice known as contact tracing. That's a challenge in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

(Image credit: Michel Lunanga)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: U.S. nuclear power deal with Saudi Arabia faces criticism in Israel
item.description: The nuclear power deal between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia was met with alarm in Israel as it deepened concerns that Israel, a major American ally, and Trump are increasingly diverging on foreign policy and security.
item.content:encoded:
The nuclear power deal between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia was met with alarm in Israel as it deepened concerns that Israel, a major American ally, and Trump are increasingly diverging on foreign policy and security.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: 'New York Times' alleges White House abuses legal system to intimidate reporters
item.description: The New York Times will argue in court Thursday that the White House is abusing the justice system to intimidate reporters over a story that angered President Trump.
item.content:encoded:
The New York Times will argue in court Thursday that the White House is abusing the justice system to intimidate reporters over a story that angered President Trump. 

(Image credit: BRENDAN SMIALOWSKI/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: These Catholic sisters want to start a new order with a difficult mission: helping clergy abuse survivors
item.description: Sisters Theresa Alethia and Danielle Lussier call themselves the Sisters of the Little Way of Beauty, Truth and Goodness. Their order plans to help victims of abuse.
item.content:encoded:
Sisters Theresa Alethia and Danielle Lussier call themselves the Sisters of the Little Way of Beauty, Truth and Goodness. Their order plans to help victims of abuse.

(Image credit: Natosha Via for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Adora Namigadde

item.title: How predominantly Black areas in 6 states have been split up by redistricting
item.description: These maps show that the redistricting battle President Trump initiated has so far had a particular impact on largely Black congressional districts in Republican-led states.
item.content:encoded:
These maps show that the redistricting battle President Trump initiated has so far had a particular impact on largely Black congressional districts in Republican-led states.

(Image credit: Rahul Mukherjee)
item.dc:creator: Rahul Mukherjee

item.title: A town renamed its festival 'AI Love Irondale Day.' Then came the comments
item.description: The online outcry over the renaming of a summer festival illustrates the unease over the data center boom in the South.
item.content:encoded:
The online outcry over the renaming of a summer festival illustrates the unease over the data center boom in the South.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: DOT cranks up its campaign to strip bike lane references from federal websites
item.description: Transportation officials have removed a growing list of safety materials that mention bike lanes from federal websites. Safety advocates warn that's undermining proven measures for reducing crashes.
item.content:encoded:
Transportation officials have removed a growing list of safety materials that mention bike lanes from federal websites. Safety advocates warn that's undermining proven measures for reducing crashes.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose
